rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Granted. No opposition has been filed, and sufficient evidence has been presented to satisfy the Court that the settlement meets the Tec-Bilt standards are met. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551--3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, August 16, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine That The Mcclatchy Companys September 26 2012 Presentation To The Trustees Is Not Privileged Or, In The Alternative, Is Subject To The Crime Fraud Exception Under Evidence Code 956, And To Permit The Depostions Of All Persons Who Participated In That Presentation
rulings.Rulings: Denied. The presentation was a privileged communication. The privilege was not waived by the presence of the other attendees who were also represented or shared an interest in the subject matter by virtue of being co-parties to the on-going litigation. Petitioner has not met his burden of showing that the elements of the crime-fraud exception are met or that the document at issue is sufficiently connected to the alleged fraud to justify eliminating the privilege. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551--3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, August 16, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Philip Shatzs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Form Interrogatory 15.1, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Granted. Petitioner/Respondent Carlos McClatchy's Objections to Respondent Co-Trustees' Petition for Order Construing Intent of Settlor of the trust for the primary benefit of James B. McClatchy ("the Objection") is a pleading for purposes of application of the discovery code to a probate action, and the Objection does deny material allegations of the Petition, at least with respect to the alleged intent of the Trustor. A full, verified response to this form interrogatory must be provided within 20 days. Carlos and his attorneys are ordered to pay $2680 to the moving co-trustee to defray his reasonable costs of presenting this motion. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551--3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, August 16, 2016.
